title: Variables and Data Types
description: Learn how to store and manage data

scenes.speech: Let's manage data with the magic boxes called variables!
scenes.title: Variables and Data Types

scenes.speech: Variables are named boxes that hold data!
scenes.title: What are Variables?
scenes.content: name = 'Alice' stores 'Alice' in a box named 'name'. You can use it again and again whenever you need it!
scenes.highlight: name = 'Alice' → Store value in a box!

scenes.title: Integers and Floats
scenes.content: Integers (int): 1, 42, -5 / Floating point (float): 3.14, -0.5. Use the right type for each calculation!

scenes.speech: The data type for handling text!
scenes.title: Strings (str)
scenes.content: 'Hello' and "World" are strings (str). Use + to join them and * to repeat!

scenes.speech: A simple type with just True or False!
scenes.title: Booleans (bool)
scenes.content: Only two values: True (yes) or False (no). Used in conditional branches!

scenes.speech: You can check and convert types!
scenes.title: Type Checking and Conversion
scenes.content: type() checks the type; int(), str(), float() convert it. Converting '123' → 123 is very practical!

scenes.speech: Learn the rules for naming variables!
scenes.title: Naming Rules
scenes.content: Use letters, numbers, and underscores (_). Case-sensitive. Use meaningful names!

scenes.speech: There's a smart way to assign!
scenes.title: Multiple Assignment
scenes.content: a, b = 1, 2 assigns multiple at once! a, b = b, a swaps two values!

scenes.title: Constants
scenes.content: By convention, write in ALL_CAPS: MAX_SIZE = 100. Makes code more readable!

scenes.speech: You've mastered variables and data types!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
